element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn nine-command verb system (pick up, use, talk to, etc.) immediately in opening sequences
Primary protagonist; establishes basic movement and interaction in Edison mansion motel
Learning to pick up, combine, and use items in puzzle solving
Separates three protagonists across three time periods; introduces temporal puzzle mechanics
Player learns to switch between Bernard (present), Hoagie (200 years past), and Laverne (200 years future)
Changes in past affect future; items age over time; complex puzzle chains span multiple timelines
Small items shared between timelines via portable toilets; larger items via timeline placement
Driving narrative goal: prevent tentacle world domination by turning off Sludge-o-Matic in past
Encounter cartoon versions of John Hancock, Thomas Jefferson, George Washington; American colonial history puzzles
Enslaved humanity under Purple Tentacle rule; reveals consequence of failure

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Mid-game (available on Commodore 64 computer in world)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional meta-content; full original 1987 Maniac Mansion playable within Day of the Tentacle

typical_relief_triggers: Solving a puzzle and progressing narrative
Switching to different character/timeline when stuck
Successful use of time travel mechanic
typical_tension_triggers: Learning new game mechanics (time travel, character switching)
Multi-stage puzzles with dependencies across timelines
Future dystopia sequences showing failure consequences
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Episodic puzzle-driven pacing with comic relief interspersed; speedruns complete in 15-50 minutes suggesting moderate complexity; tension builds through cumulative puzzle chains rather than action sequences

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Maniac Mansion game within game available but not required for completion

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory verb system and single-character navigation in motel setting; low puzzle complexity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Time travel mechanic introduction; character separation; initial temporal puzzles; moderate complexity increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multi-timeline cause-and-effect chains; coordinated puzzle sequences across past/present/future; historical figure interactions; highest complexity in final prevention of Purple Tentacle domination
notable_spikes: Chron-o-John malfunction introducing time travel complexity
First multi-timeline puzzle requiring object placement across time periods
Final sequence preventing Purple Tentacle's rise requiring coordinated past/present/future actions

notable_level_patterns.description: Game world divided into three temporal instances (past, present, future); player must manage three characters independently; progression requires understanding how changes in one timeline affect others
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Edison mansion motel structure repeated across 200-year past, immediate present, and 200-year future

notable_level_patterns.description: Puzzles span multiple timelines; solution in one era enables puzzle components in another (e.g., medical chart → American flag design → future flag costume)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tentacle disguise acquisition requiring past-to-future item progression

notable_level_patterns.description: Different characters encounter different NPCs, objects, and puzzle elements based on their timeline; encourages character-switching to uncover narrative and puzzle solutions
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Colonial America characters only accessible to Hoagie; enslaved humans and Purple Tentacle rule only visible to Laverne
